what_it_does: This file is a structured design audit and generative grammar for the ConceptV/Vi brand surface. It inventories one pitch one-pager and three landing-page mockups, then extracts visual-language rules: palette tokens, typography, geometry, component vocabulary, zone grammar, attention flow, content budgets, and audience-skeleton invariance.
observations: It separates three compression levels: vi-onepager, dual-audience parent landing (lp1), and audience-specific deep pages for developers (lp2) and architects (lp3).
Each 'finding' includes a recipe, evidence reference, emergent flag, and a prescriptive generator_rule.
Color values are pixel-sampled from raster crops and paired with inferred CSS tokens (e.g., gold ~#C8A800, dark footer #262626, red X #C44136).
It declares a strict single-typeface, single-accent-gold, single-bullet-shape, all-buttons-are-pills discipline.
It explicitly flags WIP placeholder gray blocks and red labels as author markup that must not appear in final output.
Components are listed with reuse_notes that call out invariant structure and audience-specific swap slots.
Content_samples provide character, word, and line budgets for generative text fitting.
The audience-specific pages share an invariant skeleton with only headline, subtitle, a secondary feature block, and payment framing swapped.
A custom V glyph and V-badge circle are treated as brand seals with precise placement rules.
The problem/solution dyad, capability grid, stat-as-hero, process delivery, stage timeline, pricing table, and multi-CTA close are codified as reusable zones.
It uses an 8px base spacing unit, a ~840px centered content column, and alternating warm-white/light-gray section backgrounds.
It marks some patterns as emergent (derived from inspection) versus intentionally designed.
standouts: Generator-rule orientation: every pattern is written as an instruction for a future code/design generator.
Compression/expansion axis: the same value proposition is encoded at one-slide, parent-landing, and deep-audience depths.
Audience-skeleton invariance: lp2 and lp3 are the same page with a handful of content slots swapped.
Single-hue discipline: gold is the only saturated accent; red is reserved exclusively for pricing-table exclusion marks.
Measured evidence: color tokens include median RGB values and sample counts from PIL inspection.
Custom V glyph/wordmark and V-badge circle have explicit placement and substitution rules.
Gold-border + downward-triangle pointer is the only selection/recommended state in the system.
Multi-option close: the 'Sounds good?' section offers 3-4 equal-weight CTAs rather than a single dominant action.
conventions: JSON taxonomy organized by design layer: gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, sequence.
Kebab-case names for findings, components, and zones.
Evidence is cited with shorthand strip references (lp1 strip_00, vi-onepager, etc.).
Generator rules mix descriptive observation with normative prescription ('always', 'never').
Components include a reuse_note explaining where and how they recur.
Content_samples constrain slots by characters, words, and lines.
Warm off-white backgrounds alternate with cool light-gray emphasis zones; only gold breaks the monochrome.
All interactive elements are rendered as fully rounded pills (border-radius: 50px).
Line-art icons only; no filled icons, no emoji, no abstract geometric shapes.
Lists use a single gold right-pointing chevron bullet everywhere.
concerns: Some generator rules are derived from WIP mockups containing placeholder content, so the line between observed and prescribed is thin.
Several 'only' rules (e.g., double-chevron only on parent hero, diagonal stripe only for routing) may be overfit to a single observed instance.
The single-sans-serif claim is based on raster inspection and is not confirmed by source font files.
The gold token #C8A800 is inferred from JPEG medians and may differ from the original design-system source.
The 'no other red' rule conflicts with the red placeholder labels that are present in the mockups (though flagged as WIP).
Audience-skeleton invariance claims only 3-5 swap slots, but lp3 adds value-prop cards and a software-logo strip not in lp2.
Some content_samples (e.g., section-intro-tagline) lack direct evidence mapping in the file.
References to 'Tim' appear without context or definition.
notes: A dense, rule-encoded design audit that turns one pitch slide and three web mockups into a reproducible generative grammar for ConceptV/Vi landing pages.
questions: Who is 'Tim' and what role do their comments play in the findings?
What is the canonical source of the color tokens — design files, CSS, or raster measurement?
What is the exact name or source of the single sans-serif typeface family?
Is there a vector/source file for the custom V glyph and V-badge circle?
Are lp2 and lp3 built pages or still mockups with placeholders?
How should placeholder red labels be replaced in final generated output?
What is the intended CMS or data source for stats, testimonials, and pricing values?
What do the 'carousel banner' and 'multi-device showcase' components look like in detail?
What is the relationship between this file and other files in counterpart-design/review/ingest?
Is this file meant to drive an automated generator, or is it primarily documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and actual content align: it documents the ConceptV/Vi brand surface and encodes it as a generative visual language with rules, components, and content budgets.
apparent_themes: Generative grammar extracted from static artefacts
Visual restraint through single accent, typeface, and bullet shape
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to transform a set of design mockups into a reproducible specification so that a generator can produce consistent ConceptV/Vi landing pages at different depths and for different audiences without losing the visual language.
novelty: The explicit compression axis across one-pager/parent/audience pages, the rigorous single-X discipline encoded as hard generator rules, pixel-sampled tokens paired with inferred CSS values, and content budgets tied to component slots.
contribution: It contributes a structured, tokenized inventory of the ConceptV/Vi landing design language — including color/typography/spacing rules, reusable components, content-budget constraints, audience-skeleton invariance, and generator rules — that a fused system could use to reproduce consistent layouts across compression levels and audience variants.
summary_for_embedding: A structured design-substrate audit that encodes the ConceptV/Vi one-pager and three landing-page mockups into a generative visual language with color tokens, typography rules, component vocabulary, zone grammar, content budgets, and audience-skeleton invariance.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a machine-readable generative specification for reproducing the ConceptV/Vi brand surface at multiple compression levels and for different audience variants.
